source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Suppose a search for solar neutrinos is to be mounted using a large sample of lithium enriched in the isotope $ {}_{3}^{7} \text{Li}$. Detection depends on production, separation, and detection of the electron-capturing isotope $ {}_{4}^{7} \text{Be}$ with a half-life of 53 days. The low-lying levels of these two nuclei are shown below in Fig. 2.28. The atomic mass of $ {}_{4}^{7} \text{Be}$ in its ground state lies 0.86 MeV above the atomic mass of $ {}_{3}^{7} \text{Li}$ in its ground state. To calibrate this detector, a point source emitting $10^{17}$ monochromatic neutrinos/sec with energy 1.5 MeV is placed in the center of a one metric ton sphere of lithium-7. Estimate the total equilibrium disintegration rate of the beryllium-7, given $$\begin{array}{l} G_{V}=1.42 \times 10^{-49} \mathrm{erg} \mathrm{~cm}; G_{A}=1.60 \times 10^{-49} \mathrm{erg} \mathrm{~cm}^{3}; \rho_{\mathrm{Li}}=0.53 \mathrm{gm} / \mathrm{cm}^{3}\end{array}$$.

To calibrate this detector, a point source emitting $10^{17}$ monochromatic neutrinos/sec with energy 1.5 MeV is placed in the center of a one metric ton sphere of lithium-7. Estimate the total equilibrium disintegration rate of the beryllium-7, given $$\begin{array}{l} G_{V}=1.42 \times 10^{-49} \, \mathrm{erg}\,\mathrm{cm}; \\ G_{A}=1.60 \times 10^{-49} \, \mathrm{erg}\,\mathrm{cm}^{3}; \\ \rho_{\mathrm{Li}}=0.53 \, \mathrm{gm}/\mathrm{cm}^{3} \end{array}$$.

open_ended_problem_variants.title: SeePhys 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text: Suppose a search for solar neutrinos is to be mounted using a large sample of lithium enriched in the isotope ${}_{3}^{7}\text{Li}$. Detection depends on production, separation, and detection of the electron-capturing isotope ${}_{4}^{7}\text{Be}$ with a half-life of 53 days. The low-lying levels of these two nuclei are shown in Fig. 2.28. The atomic mass of ${}_{4}^{7}\text{Be}$ in its ground state lies 0.86 MeV above the atomic mass of ${}_{3}^{7}\text{Li}$ in its ground state. To calibrate this detector, a point source emitting $10^{17}$ monochromatic neutrinos/sec with energy 1.5 MeV is placed in the center of a one metric ton sphere of lithium-7. Estimate the total equilibrium disintegration rate of the beryllium-7, given $$\begin{array}{l} G_{V}=1.42 \times 10^{-49} \, \mathrm{erg}\,\mathrm{cm}; \\ G_{A}=1.60 \times 10^{-49} \, \mathrm{erg}\,\mathrm{cm}^{3}; \\ \rho_{\mathrm{Li}}=0.53 \, \mathrm{gm}/\mathrm{cm}^{3} \end{array}$$.
